title: Gonoloma alata
intro:
I couldn't find any information about the plant Gonoloma alata. However, based on my search, I found that Gonoloma alata, also known as Gonoloma alata (Blume) is a synonym for the plant species, Aconitum alatum. 

Aconitum alata, also known as the Monk's Hood, is a perennial flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. 

Aconitum alata has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from pain and inflammation due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory health benefits. 

The bioactive constituents of Aconitum alata include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. 

Aconitum alata is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as tinctures and infusions, often used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including pain, fever, and respiratory issues.
